info.title: AOS-CX
info.description: AOS-CX is a modern and fully programmable operating system built using a database-centric design, which ensures higher availability and dynamic software process changes for reduced downtime. To make any API calls to your AOS-CX switch with this collection, set **AOS-CX ENV** as the active environment & update **switch_ip, version, username, password** variables with valid values. Here is what these variables refer to: - **Switch IP** - IP Address of the AOS-CX Switch. This IP address has to be reachable from the Postman application. Eg. [https://10.10.223.180](https://10.10.223.180) - **Version -** Version of the AOS-CX API that you would like to use. Ex. _v10.13, v10.12, latest_ - **Username & Password -** Login credentials for an admininstrator user for the AOS-CX switch. The login session cookie will be created using these credentials. Once you have set the above values in your **AOS-CX ENV**, you are ready to make API calls to your AOS-CX Switch. The Postman collection automatically handles the login step & stores the token in the **AOS-CX ENV** for future API calls. For more details, please check out [AOS-CX's guides](https://developer.arubanetworks.com/aruba-aoscx/docs/about) on [devHub](https://devhub.arubanetworks.com/home).

get.summary: Retrieve a configuration information by name
get.description:
Retrieve a configuration in JSON or CLI format, or the difference between this and another configuration.

This URI is not supported by Notifications or NAE.

parameters.name: diff
parameters.description: Configuration name for the other configuration when getting the diff, as a full URL (i.e. /rest/'version'/configs/startup-config, where 'version' matches the current API version)

put.description:
Upload a new configuration using JSON format.

This URI is not supported by Notifications or NAE.

get.description:
Retrieve a configuration in JSON format.

This URI is not supported by Notifications or NAE.

put.description:
Upload a new configuration using JSON format.

This URI is not supported by Notifications or NAE.

Ends the auto-checkpoint background timer and creates a permanent checkpoint.

This URI is not supported by Notifications or NAE.

post.description:
Set the auto-checkpoint timer interval.

This URI is not supported by Notifications or NAE.

put.description:
Copy a configuration to another one on the device.

This URI is not supported by Notifications or NAE.

parameters.name: from
parameters.description: (Required) Source configuration name as a full URL (i.e. /rest/'version'/configs/startup-config, where 'version' matches the current API version). Possible valures are 'running-config', 'startup-config' or any other checkpoint name. Having the same origin and destination results in failure. Note: it's not possible to have two checkpoints with the same configuration

get.description:
Copy local configuration (e.g., 'running-config', 'startup-config') to remote.

This URI is not supported by Notifications or NAE.

parameters.name: to
parameters.description: (Required) Destination location (e.g., tftp URL)

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: (Required) Type

parameters.name: vrf
parameters.description: (Required) VRF Name

put.description:
Copy remote configuration to local (e.g., 'running-config', 'startup-config').

This URI is not supported by Notifications or NAE.

parameters.name: from
parameters.description: (Required) Source location (e.g., tftp URL)

parameters.name: vrf
parameters.description: (Required) VRF Name

get.summary: Retrieve a configuration's hash by name
get.description:
Retrieve a configuration's hash by name and format.

This URI is not supported by Notifications or NAE.

parameters.name: format
parameters.description: Configuration format (defaults to CLI)

get.summary: Retrieve a list of current configurations
get.description:
Retrieve a list of current configurations (including running-config).

This URI is not supported by Notifications or NAE.

parameters.name: start
parameters.description: Start time in Unix Epoch format

parameters.name: end
parameters.description: End time in Unix Epoch format

parameters.name: details
parameters.description: (Required) Show configuration metadata (defaults to true)

put.description:
Copy local configurations.

This URI is not supported by Notifications or NAE.

parameters.name: from
parameters.description: (Required) Source configuration name as full URL (i.e. /rest/'version'/fullconfigs/startup-config, where 'version' matches the current API version). Possible values are 'runnig-config', 'startup-config' or any other checkpoint name. Having the same origin and destination results in failure. Note: it's not possible to have two checkpoints with the same configuration

get.description: Get current log entries.

parameters.name: If-None-Match
parameters.description: Entity-tag value for representation comparison (see RFC 7232 - Conditional Requests - section 3.2)

parameters.name: priority
parameters.description: Log priority levels. Valid values 0-7. The format is either integer or integer range(e.g. 1..3). Single integer option will match events with equal or lower severity than this value. Integer range option will match all events with priority in the range.

parameters.name: since
parameters.description: Fetch logs since the time specified. Valid format: YYYY-MM-DD hh:mm:ss. Relative words like yesterday, today, now,1 day ago, 10 hours ago, 12 minutes ago are accepted. Words like hour ago, minute ago and day ago must precede with a positive integer and can be in plural form too

parameters.name: until
parameters.description: Fetch logs until the time specified. Valid format: YYYY-MM-DD hh:mm:ss. Relative words like yesterday, today, now,1 day ago, 10 hours ago, 12 minutes ago are accepted. Words like hour ago, minute ago and day ago must precede with a positive integer and can be in plural form too

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Number of log entries in the response.Valid range is 1-1000

parameters.name: MESSAGE
parameters.description: Exact log message that is expected to be matched.

parameters.name: MESSAGE_ID
parameters.description: A comma separated list of 128-bit message identifiers for recognizing certain message types. Use MESSAGE_ID 50c0fa81c2a545ec982a54293f1b1945 and 73d7a43eaf714f97bbdf2b251b21cade to query all of the events in the systemd journal. Default value is 50c0fa81c2a545ec982a54293f1b1945.

parameters.name: ID
parameters.description: A comma separated list of event log identifiers.

parameters.name: IP
parameters.description: A comma separated list of event log filter ip addresses.

parameters.name: MAC
parameters.description: A comma separated list of event log filter mac addresses.

parameters.name: EVENT_CATEGORY
parameters.description: The event category. For retrieving only the events in a specific category.

parameters.name: SYSLOG_IDENTIFIER
parameters.description: This is the module generating the log message. Use this field to filter logs by a specific module.

parameters.name: NAE_AGENT_NAME
parameters.description: The NAE agent name. This field is only included in messages sent by NAE backend when an alert happens and indicates which agent generated that alert.

parameters.name: _PID
parameters.description: The Process ID of the process that is generating the log entry.

parameters.name: _GID
parameters.description: The Group ID of the process that is generating the log entry.

parameters.name: _UID
parameters.description: The User ID of the process that is generating the log entry.

get.description:
Get ping statistics for destination.

This URI is not supported by Notifications or NAE.

parameters.name: If-None-Match
parameters.description: Entity-tag value for representation comparison (see RFC 7232 - Conditional Requests - section 3.2)

parameters.name: ping_target
parameters.description: (Required) ping IP address

parameters.name: is_ipv4
parameters.description: (Required) true if ping_target is an IPv4 address

parameters.name: is_bcast
parameters.description: true if ping_target is a broadcast address

parameters.name: ping_data_fill
parameters.description: (Required) ping dataFill pattern in hexadecimal, and maximum pattern data less than 32

parameters.name: ping_data_size
parameters.description: set the packet size to n bytes, default value 100.

parameters.name: ping_time_out
parameters.description: ping timeout, default value 2

parameters.name: ping_interval
parameters.description: set ping interval, time between packets in seconds

parameters.name: ping_repetitions
parameters.description: send n ping signals, default value 5

parameters.name: ping_type_of_service
parameters.description: ping type of service

parameters.name: include_time_stamp
parameters.description: include time stamp

parameters.name: include_time_stamp_address
parameters.description: include time stamp address

parameters.name: record_route
parameters.description: route recorded

parameters.name: mgmt
parameters.description: VRF name/Management mutually exclusive. default value false

parameters.name: vrf_name
parameters.description: VRF name/Management mutually exclusive. default value 'swns'

parameters.name: If-None-Match
parameters.description: Entity-tag value for representation comparison (see RFC 7232 - Conditional Requests - section 3.2)

get.description:
Get traceroute result to a destination.

This URI is not supported by Notifications or NAE.

parameters.name: If-None-Match
parameters.description: Entity-tag value for representation comparison (see RFC 7232 - Conditional Requests - section 3.2)

parameters.name: ip
parameters.description: (Required) destination IP. IPv4, IPv6, or hostname

parameters.name: is_ipv4

parameters.name: loose_source_ip
parameters.description: IPv4 address. Destination IP must be IPv4.

parameters.name: destination_port
parameters.description: Integer in range 1-34000

parameters.name: max_ttl
parameters.description: maximum time-to-live (TTL), integer in range 1-255, default value 30.

parameters.name: probes
parameters.description: integer in range 1-5, default value 3

parameters.name: timeout
parameters.description: nteger in range 1-60, default value 3

parameters.name: mgmt
parameters.description: VRF name/Management mutually exclusive. default value false

parameters.name: vrf_name
parameters.description: VRF name/Management mutually exclusive. default value 'swns'
